content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers, nnn and iii. Your task is to determine whether the **ithi^{th}ith bit** (0-indexed from the least significant bit) in the **binary form of n** is 111.
Return truetruetrue if the bit is set, otherwise return falsefalsefalse.

Function DeclarationFunction NameisBitSet\text{isBitSet}isBitSet – This function checks whether the ithi^{th}ith bit (0-indexed from the least significant bit) in the binary representation of an integer nnn is set.

Parameters
nnn: An integer whose binary representation is to be examined.
iii: An integer indicating the bit position (0-indexed from the least significant bit).
Return Value
Returns truetruetrue if the ithi^{th}ith bit of nnn is set (i.e., equals 1).
Returns falsefalsefalse otherwise.
`The input and output formats given below are only if you want to test using custom inputs.`

Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105
1≤n≤3∗1091 \leq n \leq 3*10^91≤n≤3∗109
0≤i≤310 \leq i \leq 310≤i≤31

Input Format


The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.




Each test case consists of two lines containing integers:



nnn — the number
iii — the bit position to check




Output Format

For each test case, print truetruetrue if the ithi^{th}ith bit of nnn is set.
Otherwise, print falsefalsefalse.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Binary representation of 12 is 1100.
The bit at index 2 (from the right) is 1, so it is set.

Explanation:
Binary representation of 7 is 0111.
The bit at index 3 is 0, so it is not set.

Explanation:
Binary representation of 20 is 10100.
The bit at index 4 is 1, so it is set.
